title: segfault during tracing - repeated runs with default VerilatedContext
body:
Seen at ToT today, linux (ubuntu).

The verilated design is created on the stack as a local variable (technically a class member of a googletest fixture), then a loop runs the design calling `eval()` as per usual. Afterwards the test exits and everything is cleaned up (`trace_file->close()` is called too).
If the test is run again, with the same design, then I am seeing segfaults when tracing is enabled. In particular, a heap-use-after-free determined by gcc's `-fsanitize=address` at:
```
    #0 0x55c81f60458c in VerilatedModel::contextp() const /home/pieter/work/verilator/include/verilated.h:284                                                              
    #1 0x55c81f758bd1 in VerilatedTrace<VerilatedFst, VerilatedFstBuffer>::addModel(VerilatedModel*) /home/pieter/work/verilator/include/verilated_trace_imp.h:632         
```
There seems to be some cleanup happening of the static global default VerilatedContext when a design is destructed (in my case, when the stack variable goes out of scope), when tracing is enabled. If I create a new VerilatedContext and pass it to the design's constructor, there is no issue and things run fine.

I am verilating the design with CMake, the `verilate` macro, with no additional args or flags other than `--trace-fst`. VCD tracing behaves the same way. And no issues using the default context when not tracing.
